schema_note: WHOOP 5.0 (Maverick) protocol schema. Mirrors the 4.0 whoop_protocol.json top-level layout (version/enums/envelope/packets) so the Phase 5 Swift (Schema.swift / WhoopProtocol) and Python (whoop_protocol) loaders stay compatible. This envelope describes the Maverick OUTER WRAPPER (NOT the 4.0 inner frame). Enums are the four r52 maps copied VERBATIM from the 4.0 schema (WG50_r52 confirmed identical, RESEARCH 'Don't Hand-Roll'). Packets carry body field maps decoded in Phase 4. OFFSET BASE (load-bearing): every packet field 'off' is BODY-ABSOLUTE, i.e. an index into the flat body returned by strip_maverick() (frame[4:4+length]). The body prefix is body[0]=role, body[1:4]=session token, body[4]=packet_type, body[5]=seq, body[6]=cmd, body[7:]=payload. So payload[N] == body[7+N]. The 4.0 schema offsets were FRAME-relative (4.0 REALTIME_DATA timestamp at frame off 6); the 5.0 offsets below are BODY-relative and were reconciled empirically in Plan 04 (see per-packet notes). Every field is tagged with epoch ('unix' vs 'device' vs 'none'), note (provenance), and confidence (VERIFIED/HYPOTHESIS) per SCHEMA-02.
framing_notes: Maverick outer wrapper, empirically verified on 5028 captured ATT frames across two sessions (Phase 1 + Phase 2). Layout: [0xAA SOF][0x01 version][length u16-LE][body (length bytes, FLAT, body[0]==role)][trailer 4B]. total_len == length + 8 (4-byte header + length-byte body + 4-byte trailer) for 5028/5028 frames. The body is FLAT — it is NOT a nested 0xAA 4.0 frame (RESEARCH Finding 5). The 4.0 inner CRC gate fails 0% on all 5028 frames; the 4-byte trailer's checksum algorithm is OPEN (RESEARCH Finding 6): standard CRC16/CRC32 variants ruled out by exhaustive negative test.

envelope.name: length
envelope.note: u16-LE body length; total_len == length + 8 for 5028/5028 frames

envelope.name: body
envelope.note: flat body region; frame[4:4+length]. body[0]==role. length bytes total including role. NOT a nested 0xAA frame (Finding 5). Packet body field maps below are indexed BODY-ABSOLUTE against this region.

envelope.name: trailer
envelope.note: checksum algorithm OPEN; standard CRC16/CRC32 variants ruled out (Finding 6). Likely non-standard checksum or computed over a transformed input.

fields.name: packet_type
fields.note: body[4] == 36 COMMAND_RESPONSE (Plan 03 command_surface_5.py over the 5028-frame corpus)

fields.name: seq
fields.note: monotonic sequence; request->response pairing by seq (A7, 89/106 in-burst MATCHes, Plan 03)

fields.name: resp_cmd
fields.note: body[6] CommandNumber being responded to. 10 OBSERVED commands (3,22,23,34,69,117,118,120,141,145); resolved via r52 CommandNumber (Plan 03)

fields.name: payload
fields.note: command-specific response payload (body[7:]). For GET_DATA_RANGE (cmd 34) it carries unix-epoch u32-LE timestamps — see unix_range_start below.

fields.name: unix_range_start
fields.note: PROTO-15 unix epoch. GET_DATA_RANGE (cmd 34) response payload carries u32-LE Unix seconds (19 candidates in corpus; headline 2026-05-08, decode_5.scan_unix_timestamps). Offset within payload is scanned (1.4e9..1.9e9 window); representative body offset shown. epoch=unix.

COMMAND_RESPONSE.note: COMMAND (35) shares this body prefix layout (role/token/type/seq/cmd); the cmd-in WRITE direction. Field offsets are BODY-ABSOLUTE (payload starts body[7]).

fields.name: packet_type
fields.note: body[4] == 48 EVENT (Plan 03 decode_streams_5.py, 136 EVENT frames resolved)

fields.name: seq
fields.note: monotonic sequence

fields.name: event
fields.note: body[6] r52 EventNumber. OBSERVED: STRAP_CONDITION_REPORT(29 via remap), BLE_CONNECTION_UP/DOWN, BLE_REALTIME_HR_ON/OFF, BATTERY_LEVEL(3), EXTENDED_BATTERY_INFORMATION(63). Three unmapped (110/120/123) surfaced as 5.0-new candidates (Plan 03)

fields.name: event_timestamp
fields.note: PROTO-15 DEVICE epoch (NOT unix). u32-LE at body[8] == payload[1]. 136 device-epoch values decoded (e.g. 1780152939). epoch=device — distinct from the GET_DATA_RANGE unix epoch (decode_5.parse_body_5)

EVENT.note: EVENT carries a DEVICE-epoch u32 at body[8], whereas COMMAND_RESPONSE GET_DATA_RANGE carries a UNIX-epoch u32 — the PROTO-15 dual-epoch model. Field offsets BODY-ABSOLUTE.

fields.name: event
fields.note: body[6] == 3 BATTERY_LEVEL (or 63 EXTENDED_BATTERY_INFORMATION). The event itself is VERIFIED-present; the SOC byte layout below is HYPOTHESIS (Plan 03)

fields.name: event_timestamp
fields.note: device-epoch u32 at body[8] (same as generic EVENT)

fields.name: state_of_charge_x10
fields.note: 4.0 A6 layout: SOC x10 as u16. On 5.0 NO candidate u16 in the BATTERY_LEVEL/EXTENDED payload cleanly resolves to the CONFIRMED 0x2A19=23% read (Plan 03). HYPOTHESIS pending a dedicated GET_BATTERY_LEVEL (cmd 26) capture in Phase 5. Offset overlaps event_timestamp by design (4.0 candidate layout) — not yet disambiguated.

fields.name: millivolts
fields.note: 4.0 A6 layout: battery mV as u16 at event off 5. HYPOTHESIS on 5.0 — not cross-validated against ground truth (Plan 03).

EVENT_BATTERY_LEVEL.note: Battery event located and decoded but the SOC offset is HYPOTHESIS: the 4.0 A6 layout does not cleanly produce 23% on 5.0. Dedicated GET_BATTERY_LEVEL capture flagged for Phase 5. Field offsets BODY-ABSOLUTE.

fields.name: packet_type
fields.note: body[4] == 49 METADATA

fields.name: seq
fields.note: monotonic sequence

fields.name: meta_type
fields.note: body[6] r52 MetadataType. OBSERVED: HISTORY_START(73 frames), HISTORY_END(79), HISTORY_COMPLETE(2) — the historical-offload framing markers (Plan 03 decode_streams_5.py)

fields.name: payload
fields.note: HISTORY_END payload (4.0 precedent <LHLL): [unix u32][subsec u16][unk u32][trim u32]. On 5.0 the offload is documented-only (D-09); live-test deferred to Phase 5 (PROTO-10).

METADATA.note: METADATA brackets the historical-offload stream (HISTORY_START/END/COMPLETE). Field offsets BODY-ABSOLUTE.

fields.name: packet_type
fields.note: body[4] == 50 CONSOLE_LOGS

fields.name: seq
fields.note: monotonic sequence

fields.name: log_text
fields.note: ASCII firmware console narration. Plan 03 scrubbed digit-runs (T-04-04) and surfaced only protocol-structure narration + the hex trim cursor 0x00000004:000130ef (the store-then-ack persistence pointer for historical offload, PROTO-10). No device identity committed.

CONSOLE_LOGS.note: Firmware console log stream — narration scrubbed for safe documentation (digit-run redaction). Field offsets BODY-ABSOLUTE.

fields.name: packet_type
fields.note: body[4] == 40 REALTIME_DATA. 159 frames decoded in Plan 04 (decode_biometrics_5.py)

fields.name: seq
fields.note: monotonic sequence

fields.name: subseq
fields.note: body[6] is a per-frame SUB-SEQUENCE counter (41-243 monotonic), NOT a CommandNumber for this stream (Plan 04 empirical finding)

fields.name: device_timestamp
fields.note: device-epoch u32 at payload[1:5] == body[8:12] (Plan 04 empirical reconciliation). epoch=device.

fields.name: heart_rate
fields.note: bpm. HR @ payload[5] == body[12] (EMPIRICALLY reconciled — the literal 4.0 offset decodes to all-zero/0x01 on 5.0). Smooth physiological series 84-131 bpm, mean 102, across 159 frames. PROTO-07 VERIFIED. Cross-validated by standard 0x2A37 parse_hr (D-08 substitute: RR<->HR self-consistency 8/8).

fields.name: rr_count
fields.note: number of RR intervals following. rr_count @ payload[6] == body[13] (Plan 04)

fields.name: rr_interval
fields.note: RR interval(s) in ms, uint16-LE, repeated rr_count times from payload[7:] == body[14:]. 8/8 RR-bearing frames self-consistent with 60000/HR (e.g. HR=91 -> RR 645-694 ms). PROTO-07 VERIFIED.

REALTIME_DATA.note: 5.0 REALTIME_DATA (type 40) layout was reconciled EMPIRICALLY in Plan 04, NOT from the literal 4.0 frame offsets. Payload base is body[7]: device-epoch @ payload[1:5], HR @ payload[5], rr_count @ payload[6], RR @ payload[7:]. Field offsets shown BODY-ABSOLUTE.

fields.name: packet_type
fields.note: body[4] == 43 REALTIME_RAW_DATA. NOT OBSERVED in the Phase 4 captures (Plan 04: raw_imu_present=false; START_RAW_DATA cmd 81 / TOGGLE_IMU_MODE cmd 106 never triggered). r52-expected from the PacketType enum.

fields.name: seq
fields.note: monotonic sequence (template from 4.0; type 43 absent from 5.0 captures)

fields.name: device_timestamp
fields.note: device-epoch u32 (4.0 Gen4 layout template, frame off 11 -> body-absolute estimate). HYPOTHESIS: type 43 not captured on 5.0 (Plan 04).

fields.name: heart_rate
fields.note: 4.0 Gen4 IMU-variant HR header (frame off 21 template). HYPOTHESIS on 5.0 (type 43 absent).

1917.note: Gen4-VERIFIED 4.0 layout kept as a DECODE-READY TEMPLATE for 5.0 — type 43 was NOT observed in the Phase 4 D-05 capture (raw_imu_present=false). NO 5.0 offsets fabricated (Pitfall 5). Apply ONLY when a real type-43 frame appears; needs a TOGGLE_IMU_MODE/START_RAW_DATA capture (Phase 5) to grade VERIFIED. PROTO-14 HYPOTHESIS. WR-03 reference frame: axis offsets in this schema are BODY-ABSOLUTE (apply against raw body bytes). The Python decoder in decode_biometrics_5.py uses PAYLOAD-RELATIVE offsets (subtract 7 = the 7-byte body prefix: role[1]+token[3]+ptype[1]+seq[1]+subseq[1]). Both reference the same physical bytes; body-absolute = payload-relative + 7.

REALTIME_RAW_DATA.note: PROTO-14 IMU: HYPOTHESIS. Type 43 absent from the 5.0 captures; the 4.0 Gen4-VERIFIED layout is a decode-ready template only. No 5.0 offsets fabricated.

biometric_verdicts.note: Per-stream biometric confidence map (PROTO-07/11/12/13/14, Plan 04 decode_biometrics_5.py). Streams without on-wire bytes are HYPOTHESIS with 4.0-cloud-computed provenance — no schema packet entries are fabricated for them (Pitfall 5 / T-04-05).

HR_RR.provenance: standard 0x2A37 parse_hr verbatim + custom type-40 reconciled (HR @ payload[5], RR @ payload[7:]); RR<->HR self-consistent 8/8 frames

IMU.provenance: type 43 absent (raw_imu_present=false); 4.0 Gen4 template ready; needs TOGGLE_IMU_MODE capture

SpO2.provenance: type 53 byte not observed; 4.0 = cloud-computed off-wire; no fabricated offset

skin_temp.provenance: event 17 TEMPERATURE_LEVEL not observed; 4.0 never captured it

respiration.provenance: no respiration field on the wire; likely derived/sleep metric, may be cloud-only
